STAYCRAFTCRAFTING YOUR NEXT ESCAPE
PACKAGE 01 / KERALA
Beach & Waterfront Stay

Vembanad
Backwater Bliss

Beach sunsets, palm-fringed backwaters and serene shikkara cruises on India's longest lake.

3 Days / 2 Nights
Alleppey, Kerala
From ₹5,500
The Experience
Where the sea meets the backwaters

Experience the stunning beauty of Vembanad Lake, the longest lake in India and the heart of Kerala's backwaters. Located near Alleppey (Alappuzha), this getaway blends a relaxing beach-house stay, a peaceful backwater resort or villa, and unforgettable water activities — from golden beach sunsets to refreshing sunrise kayaking.

Beach House

A breezy night by the shore with Arabian Sea sunsets.

Shikkara Cruise

Two hours gliding across calm Vembanad Lake.

Sunrise Kayaking

Paddle the backwaters at dawn.

Day by Day
Your 3-Day Journey
DAY01
Arrival & Beachside SunsetBEACH HOUSE STAY
  • 11:30 AMPickup by private cab at Alleppey
  • 12:00 PMBeach house check-in
  • 01:30 PMNadan food spot visit — authentic Kerala flavours
  • 04:00 PMVTV Church visit
  • 05:30 PMReturn to beach house
  • 06:30 PMBeach sunset experience
    Overnight at Beach House
DAY02
Sightseeing & Backwater CruiseRESORT / VILLA STAY
  • 07:30 AMWake-up & refreshments
  • 09:00 AMCheck-out
  • 09:30 AMBreakfast at restaurant
  • 10:30 AMAlleppey Lighthouse
  • 11:30 AMArt Museum visit
  • 12:30 PMBackwaters resort / villa check-in
  • 03:30 PMShikkara boating at Vembanad Lake
  • 06:00 PMTea / coffee with snacks
  • 08:30 PMDinner
    Overnight at Backwaters Resort / Villa
DAY03
Sunrise Kayaking & FarewellDEPARTURE
  • 05:30 AMWake-up & refreshments
  • 06:00 AMSunrise kayaking on the backwaters
  • 09:00 AMBreakfast
  • 10:30 AMCheck-out
  • 11:30 AMDrop at Alleppey Railway Station
A Glimpse
Stays, Views & Moments

From beachfront verandahs and garden villas to poolside afternoons and backwater reflections — a taste of where you'll stay and what you'll see.

Taste of Nadan Kerala

Day 1 begins at a local Nadan food spot — traditional Kerala home-style cooking: appam, spicy curries, fresh seafood and sweet payasam. Press play to take the tour.

Open on YouTube ↗
Best Food
What to Eat in Alleppey

Coastal Kerala cooking at its finest — fresh backwater fish, coconut-rich curries and the legendary banana-leaf sadya.

VEG

Kerala Sadya

Grand vegetarian banana-leaf feast — rice, sambar, avial, thoran, olan and payasam.

NON-VEG

Karimeen Pollichathu

Pearlspot fish marinated in spices, wrapped in banana leaf and pan-grilled.

VEG

Appam & Stew

Soft lacy rice hoppers with a mildly spiced coconut-milk vegetable or meat stew.

NON-VEG

Kerala Fish Curry

Tangy red fish curry with kudampuli (gambooge) and coconut — a backwater classic.

SPECIAL

Payasam

Creamy traditional dessert of rice or vermicelli simmered in jaggery & coconut milk.

SPECIAL

Tender Coconut

Fresh, chilled karikku straight off the palm — the perfect backwater refresher.

Best Time to Visit
Weather & Seasons
PEAK
Winter · Best
Oct – Feb
17–32°C
Cool, dry & calm backwaters — ideal for cruises, beach evenings & kayaking.
Summer
Mar – May
25–37°C
Hot & humid; quieter and cheaper, with early-morning or evening cruises best.
Monsoon
Jun – Sep
23–30°C
Lush green & dramatic skies, fewer tourists & the famous snake-boat races.

Our pick: Plan for November–February — calm backwaters, cool dry air and the most comfortable conditions for shikkara cruising and sunrise kayaking.

JUNGLE MEET ADVENTURESWANDER INTO UNSEEN LANDSCAPES
PACKAGE 02 / KARNATAKA
Adventure Package

Chikamagalur
Coffee Country

The Coffee Land of Karnataka — misty peaks, hidden waterfalls, jeep safaris and campfire nights.

3 Days / 2 Nights
Chikamagalur, Karnataka
From ₹6,500
The Experience
India's coffee country awaits

Chikamagalur's cool climate and rolling hills make it ideal for coffee cultivation, inviting you to explore estates and savour freshly brewed coffee. Home to Mullayanagiri — the highest peak in Karnataka — and the forests and wildlife sanctuaries around Kemmangundi, it's a must-visit for a peaceful escape or an adventurous getaway.

Peak Hikes

Mullayanagiri, Z Point & Baba Budan Giri viewpoints.

Jeep Safaris

Off-road to Jhari & Hebbe waterfalls.

Campfire Night

Evenings under the stars (weather permitting).

Day by Day
Your 3-Day Adventure
DAY01
Peaks, Waterfalls & CoffeeHOMESTAY · CAMPFIRE NIGHT
  • 07:00 AMArrival & pickup by cab · refreshment
  • 09:00 AMBreakfast, then start sightseeing
  • 10:30 AMMullayanagiri Peak
  • 11:30 AMSitalayanagiri View Point
  • 12:00 PMHonnamana Halla Falls
  • 12:30 PMJeep safari to Jhari Waterfalls + bathing
  • 02:30 PMBaba Budan Giri
  • 03:30 PMZ Point short hike & view point
  • 04:30 PMSiri Cafe — Chikamagalur coffee
  • 07:30 PMCampfire, then dinner
    Overnight at Homestay
DAY02
Kemmangundi & Hebbe FallsHOMESTAY STAY
  • 06:30 AMWake up · tea/coffee · breakfast
  • 08:00 AMCheck-out · head to Kemmangundi
  • 10:30 AMJeep safari to waterfalls
  • 11:15 AMShort jungle trek
  • 11:30 AMHebbe Waterfalls visit
  • 12:30 PMZ Point view
  • 01:00 PMKemmangundi Peak & Park
  • 08:00 PMDinner
    Overnight at Homestay
DAY03
Water Sports & HeritageDEPARTURE
  • 07:30 AMTea/coffee · breakfast · check-out
  • 10:30 AMYagachi Dam — adventure water activities
  • 12:30 PMBelur Shri Chennakeshava Temple
  • 02:30 PMHalebidu archaeological site
  • 04:00 PMDrop at Chikmagalur / Belur Bus Stand
A Glimpse
Homestay & Highlands

Forest-edge homestays with high wooden ceilings, valley-view balconies and breezy dining halls — your base between the peaks and waterfalls.

Watch the Adventure

Get a feel for the misty peaks, hidden waterfalls and coffee-country trails before you arrive — press play to see the Chikamagalur experience in motion.

Open on YouTube ↗
Best Food
Taste of Malnad

Earthy Western-Ghats cooking built on rice, coconut, jackfruit and forest spices — plus the region's world-famous estate coffee.

VEG

Akki Roti

Soft rice-flour flatbread pressed by hand, served with coconut or spicy chutney.

SPECIAL

Malnad Filter Coffee

Slow-brewed estate-grown decoction with hot milk — Chikamagalur in a tumbler.

NON-VEG

Pandi Curry

Coorg-style dark, spicy pork curry simmered with kachampuli — rich and earthy.

VEG

Bisi Bele Bath

Hearty hot rice-and-lentil dish with tamarind, veggies and a fragrant masala.

VEG

Pathrode

Colocasia leaves layered with spiced rice batter, rolled, steamed and crisped.

VEG

Kadubu

Steamed dumplings of pumpkin or jackfruit — mildly sweet Malnad festival treat.

Best Time to Visit
Weather & Seasons
PEAK
Winter · Best
Oct – Mar
14–32°C
Cool, misty mornings & warm sunny days — perfect for peaks, treks & coffee walks.
Summer
Apr – May
20–35°C
Pleasant for a hill town; coffee blossoms scent the air and crowds thin out.
Monsoon
Jun – Sep
19–26°C
Heavy rain, swollen waterfalls at their best; trekking can be slippery.

Our pick: Head up between October and March — misty mornings, warm sunny afternoons and crisp nights, perfect for peak hikes and coffee-estate walks.

JUNGLE MEET ADVENTURESWANDER INTO UNSEEN LANDSCAPES
PACKAGE 03 / KERALA
Deluxe Hill-Station Package

Vagamon
Misty Meadows

Tea-garden meadows, murmuring pine forests, hidden waterfalls and an off-road jeep safari in Kerala's high hills.

3 Days / 2 Nights
Idukki, Kerala
From ₹4,750
The Experience
A hill station in the clouds

Vagamon sits on the Kottayam–Idukki border of Kerala, 1,200 metres above sea level and wrapped in the greenery of tea gardens, fresh cool air, murmuring pine forests, small waterfalls and rolling meadows. Reaching Vagamon itself is an extraordinary experience — and once you arrive, the views only get better.

Tea-Garden Meadows

Endless green meadows and tea estates to wander.

Pine Forests

Walk the famous murmuring pine woods.

Off-road Safari

Uluppuni jeep trail to hidden viewpoints.

Day by Day
Your 3-Day Hill Escape
DAY01
Meadows, Pines & CampfireCOTTAGE · CAMPFIRE NIGHT
  • 11:00 AMArrival at Vagamon · cottage check-in
  • 12:30 PMLunch
  • 01:30 PMVagamon Meadows
  • 02:30 PMPine Forest visit
  • 03:30 PMThangalpara visit
  • 04:30 PMKurishumala short hike
  • 06:00 PMTea / coffee
  • 08:00 PMDinner, then campfire
    Overnight at Cottage
DAY02
Waterfalls & Illikkal KalluCOTTAGE STAY
  • 07:00 AMTea/coffee · refreshment · breakfast
  • 09:30 AMHead to Marmala Waterfalls
  • 10:00 AMTea plantation photography
  • 10:30 AMLemon Grass Valley viewpoint
  • 11:00 AMWaterfalls visit & relax
  • 12:00 PMIllikkal Kallu visit
  • 02:00 PMBack to cottage
    Overnight at Cottage
DAY03
Off-road Safari & Dam ViewsDEPARTURE
  • 07:30 AMTea/coffee · breakfast · check-out
  • 10:00 AMUluppuni off-road jeep safari & viewpoints
  • 11:00 AMIdukki Dam viewpoint
  • 11:30 AMTunnel & waterfalls visit
  • 12:30 PMGreen Valley viewpoint
  • 01:30 PMDrop at Vagamon
A Glimpse
Cottages & Tea-Garden Views

Deluxe rooms and balconies opening straight onto the tea gardens, lakes and rolling hills of Vagamon — your home base in the clouds.

Watch the Hills Come Alive

See Vagamon's misty meadows, pine forests and off-road trails in motion before you go — press play for a quick look at the journey that awaits.

Open on YouTube ↗
Best Food
Highland Kerala Flavours

Hearty hill-station cooking — Kerala classics with a cool-climate twist, finished with tea grown on Vagamon's own slopes.

VEG

Kerala Sadya

Festive vegetarian feast on a banana leaf — the highland version of the Kerala classic.

NON-VEG

Beef Ularthiyathu

Slow-roasted Kerala beef fry with coconut slivers & pepper — best with parotta.

VEG

Appam & Stew

Lacy rice hoppers with coconut-milk stew — a warming hill-station breakfast.

SPECIAL

Estate Tea

Fresh garden tea grown on Vagamon’s own slopes — sip it with the mist.

NON-VEG

Tapioca & Fish

Steamed kappa with spicy fish curry — the soul food of the Kerala hills.

SPECIAL

Banana Fritters

Hot pazham pori — ripe Nendran banana dipped in batter and fried golden.

Best Time to Visit
Weather & Seasons
PEAK
Winter · Best
Oct – Feb
10–25°C
Cool, misty & magical — meadows fresh and green, ideal for walks & campfires.
Summer
Mar – May
15–30°C
Mild for a Kerala summer; clear valley views, great for paragliding & treks.
Monsoon
Jun – Sep
15–22°C
Intense green, low clouds & waterfalls everywhere; trails get wet & slippery.

Our pick: Visit October–February for cool, misty days (10–25°C) when the meadows are greenest — pack a light jacket for the chilly nights.
